mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns show no statistical correlation with FEDFUNDS changes, or Rank IC falls below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that immediately trigger negative equity repricing within one week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns remain positive in over 55% of instances where UNRATE rises above the 0.3pp threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates robust aggregate demand that sustains corporate earnings growth, driving positive equity returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to produce positive 60-day forward returns in at least 60% of observed cases.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during low-inflation expansions reflects supply-side strength that boosts corporate profit margins, yielding positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration produces negative 20-day forward returns in over 50% of low-inflation expansion periods.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly gains in total nonfarm payrolls directly correlate with rising consumer spending capacity, generating positive short-term equity momentum over the following week.
failure_condition: PAYEMS month-over-month changes show zero predictive power for 5-day forward returns, with Rank IC consistently below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising real GDP and flat industrial production indicates demand outpacing supply capacity, creating inflationary expectations that compress equity valuations over a quarter.
failure_condition: Positive GDPC1 growth combined with flat INDPRO fails to predict negative 60-day returns, with accuracy below chance levels.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate exceeding 2.5% triggers a repricing of growth equity valuations as borrowing costs rise, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns remain positive in over 60% of periods where FEDFUNDS exceeds the 2.5% threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Declining unemployment during periods of weak housing starts signals labor market fragility masked by statistical noise, leading to negative equity repricing within one week.
failure_condition: Falling UNRATE alongside weak HOUST fails to generate negative 5-day forward returns in at least 55% of post-recession recovery observations.
